title: X-ray Crystallography (single-crystal diffraction)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence boilerplate. Physics grounded in Rupp 2010 Biomolecular Crystallography; Giacovazzo et al. 2011 Fundamentals; Jeffrey 1997 Macromolecular Crystallography; Karplus-Diederichs 2012 data-quality metrics.

E.description: Monochromatic X-rays (typically 1.0-1.8 Å, from synchrotron or rotating anode) diffract off a single crystal; Bragg peaks at angles satisfying n*lambda = 2*d*sin(theta); intensities I(h,k,l) = |F(h,k,l)|^2 where F is Fourier transform of electron density. Phase problem requires SAD/MAD/MIR/MR methods; solvent flattening + refinement.
E.world_state_x: Electron density rho(x,y,z) in unit cell
E.observation_y: Bragg-peak integrated intensities I(h,k,l) for reciprocal-lattice points
physical_parameters_theta: Crystal unit-cell parameters (a, b, c, alpha, beta, gamma)

W.uniqueness: up to enantiomorph + phase choice (resolved by experimental phasing or MR)
W.regime: Phase problem fundamentally ill-posed from intensities alone; experimental phasing (MAD/SAD) adds constraints. Well-posed at atomic resolution (<1.2 Å) via direct methods.

C.solver_class: Direct methods (SHELX), MIR/MAD phasing, molecular replacement (Phaser), density modification (DM), learned (AlphaFold-priors)
C.error_bound: R_work ~ sqrt(sigma^2 / |F|^2); R_free independent measure; atomic-position uncertainty ~ R/sqrt(N_obs/N_param)
C.complexity: Direct methods O(N_atoms^1.5); SAD phasing O(N_atoms * N_refl); DM iteration O(N_refl * log)
